title: Chat assistant stream-event envelopes
description:
Authoritative schema for every payload the orchestrator emits via `controller.add_data(...)` on the assistant_stream `2:`-prefixed channel. This file is the SINGLE source of truth: the backend Python validator imports it, the frontend TypeScript types are generated from it, and the pipeline-fitness audit cross-checks every emit/handle site against it. A new event type without a schema entry is a build-time error; an emitted payload that fails schema validation is a 500 in DEBUG and a `stream_event_invalid` telemetry span in prod.

Maps to Constitution Principle VII (Verified Work) — every emit point must be checkable from a written contract — and Principle IV (Credential Scoping & Log Redaction) for the redactor-coverage assertion on `tool_result`. Each event lists the FRs / tasks that govern it so a reader can navigate from contract to spec to implementation.

type.description: Discriminator. The closed enum is the contract — the audit fails if a backend emit site uses a value not listed here OR if the frontend handles a value not listed here.

oneOf.title: MessageId
oneOf.description: Persistence anchor. Emitted exactly once at the start of every assistant turn so the frontend can tie streamed deltas to the saved message row.

oneOf.title: ToolUse
oneOf.description: Agent invoked an MCP tool. Frontend surfaces these as a thinking-step chip.

oneOf.title: ToolResult
oneOf.description: Tool returned. Principle IV: `content` MUST run through `audit_service.redact()` before this event is emitted; the redactor-coverage fitness function asserts this.

content.description: Redacted tool output, truncated to 1500 chars.

oneOf.title: InstanceProvisioning
oneOf.description: T070 — Container-mode chat is bringing up its instance. Frontend renders a non-blocking starting-up banner. ETA is the SC-002 cold-path budget.

oneOf.title: InstanceFailed
oneOf.description: T080 — Instance reached terminal `failed` state. Frontend renders a card with Retry + Main Menu actions per CLAUDE.md No Dead Ends.

oneOf.title: InstanceLimitExceeded
oneOf.description: T044 — Either the user is at their per-user cap (`per_user_cap` variant; FR-030a) or the platform is at capacity (`platform_capacity` variant; FR-030). The two MUST stay distinct so the UI can render different copy.

oneOf.title: InstanceUpstreamDegraded
oneOf.description: T084 — Per-message poll of the Redis upstream-state cache. Renders a non-blocking pill in the chat. FR-027a strict: instance status NEVER flipped to `failed` regardless.

capabilities.description: Map of capability name (e.g. preview_url) to upstream identifier (e.g. cloudflare).

oneOf.title: InstanceBusy
oneOf.description: T037a — A second concurrent message hit the per-instance lock and could not acquire it within the patience window. Frontend shows an amber pill, no destructive UX.

oneOf.title: InstanceTerminating
oneOf.description: T073 — User confirmed end-session. Frontend shows a teardown indicator; instance row will flip to `destroyed` on the worker job's completion.

oneOf.title: InstanceRetryStarted
oneOf.description: T080 — User tapped Retry on a failed-provision card. Frontend shows a green pill while the new provision is in-flight.

oneOf.title: Confirm
oneOf.description: Generic two-step destructive-confirm prompt. Frontend renders ConfirmCard with Cancel mandatory (CLAUDE.md No Dead Ends).

CardAction.title: CardAction
CardAction.description: A single button on a card. `action_id` either looks like `<verb>:<id>` (the chat-text protocol the assistant_endpoint switch matches on) OR an internal `link` URL the frontend follows directly.
